title: Backend Developer (Python)
description:
About Anderson, Evans and Robinson
We are a leading enterprise software architecture consultancy helping organizations modernize critical technology stacks while ensuring compliance and business continuity. Specializing in cloud-native value-added solutions for regulated industries, we design and deploy infrastructure that drives measurable ROI through scalability and resilience. Our mission is to transform how businesses interact with technology by delivering architectures that solve real-world challenges.

Working Conditions & Perks
Hybrid work model: 2 days onsite in our London office, 3 days remote with flexible hours
Competitive salary with annual performance-based bonuses
$2,000 annual learning budget for certifications, tools, and courses
Comprehensive benefits including 401(k) with 50% employer match, parental leave, and mental health support
Professional development stipend for conference attendance and projects
Health insurance and retirement plan coverage

Requirements (Must-Have)
3+ years of production-grade Python experience with at least two major frameworks (e.g., Django, Flask)
Strong practical knowledge of cloud infrastructure (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and container orchestration (Docker, Kubernetes)
Proven experience designing and implementing scalable microservices architectures
Proficiency in Git, CI/CD pipelines (Jenkins/GitHub Actions), and performance monitoring tools

Nice-to-Have
Experience with database technologies (PostgreSQL, MongoDB) and caching (Redis)
Familiarity with infrastructure as code (Terraform) and security practices (OAuth, encryption) 

Responsibilities
Design, develop, and test backend services using Python to meet client requirements for regulated environments
Collaborate with frontend teams and stakeholders to translate business needs into technical specifications
Monitor production systems for performance, scalability, and reliability with actionable reporting
Implement and maintain CI/CD pipelines to ensure seamless deployments and rapid feedback cycles

title: Frontend Developer (React)
description:
About Brown, Lowe and Patterson
Brown, Lowe and Patterson is a B2B SaaS company specializing in developer productivity tools for complex data schema management. We help engineering teams streamline API development and reduce integration friction by providing intuitive, visual schema design and validation workflows. Our mission is to eliminate the manual, error-prone processes that slow down data pipeline construction in modern software.

Working Conditions & Perks
- Hybrid work model: 2 days onsite at our New York headquarters, 3 days remote
- Flexible scheduling with core collaboration hours from 9 AM to 12 PM EST
- Competitive base salary + annual bonus structured around performance metrics
- $2,000 annual learning budget for courses, tools, and conference attendance
- Comprehensive health, dental, and vision benefits with 401(k) matching
- 4 weeks of paid time off after 12 months

Requirements (Must-Have)
- 3+ years professional experience building production-grade applications with React
- Proficiency in TypeScript for component development and type-safe APIs
- Strong understanding of schema design principles and data modeling best practices
- Experience implementing state management with Redux or similar patterns
- Familiarity with modern deployment pipelines using Vercel or equivalent
- Ability to ship high-quality features with measurable performance improvements

Nice-to-Have
- Experience with schema validation libraries like Zod or Ajv
- Knowledge of CI/CD pipelines for frontend applications

Responsibilities
- Build and maintain reusable React component libraries for schema visualization and editing tools
- Collaborate with backend engineers to define and implement RESTful schema API endpoints
- Write comprehensive unit tests using Jest to ensure component stability and edge-case coverage
- Optimize component performance and reduce bundle sizes for complex schema interactions
- Document component usage and schema validation logic for internal developer adoption
- Implement feature flags and A/B testing for new schema visualization patterns

title: Data Analyst
description:
About Robinson-Diaz
Robinson-Diaz is a leading provider of intuitive e-services for mid-market businesses, specializing in seamless customer interaction platforms that drive measurable growth and retention. We empower enterprises to transform complex service data into actionable insights through user-centric digital solutions within the competitive SaaS landscape.

Working Conditions & Perks
- Hybrid work model: 2 days onsite in our downtown office, 3 days remote
- Flexible work hours with core collaboration windows
- Competitive annual salary with bonus structure tied to individual and team performance
- $5,000 annual learning budget for certifications, courses, and tool subscriptions
- Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance
- 401(k) with company match up to 6% of salary

Requirements (Must-Have)
- 2+ years of hands-on experience as a Data Analyst in SaaS or similar environments
- Proficiency in SQL for complex data extraction, transformation, and querying
- Experience with Python or R for data manipulation, analysis, and basic modeling
- Strong practical skills in Tableau or Power BI for building production-grade dashboards
- Demonstrated ability to translate business requirements into data solutions
- Working knowledge of cloud data platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) for data storage and processing

Nice-to-Have
- Experience with cloud data warehousing solutions (Snowflake, BigQuery, or Redshift)
- Knowledge of data pipeline implementation and ETL processes
- Familiarity with agile development methodologies and cross-functional collaboration
- Background in customer analytics or marketing intelligence

Responsibilities
- Analyze customer service interaction data to identify improvement opportunities and trends
- Build and maintain real-time dashboards in Tableau tracking platform health metrics and KPIs
- Collaborate with product and engineering teams to define data requirements for new features
- Clean, transform, and aggregate data sources to support accurate reporting and decision-making
- Conduct root cause analysis on service disruptions using data and present actionable insights
- Help design and implement automated reporting pipelines for timely stakeholder intelligence

title: Data Scientist
description:
About Nelson LLC
Nelson LLC is a precision agriculture technology company that helps farmers optimize crop yields and resource use through data-driven insights. We empower agricultural operations with scalable analytics platforms that integrate weather, soil, and field data to support sustainable farming decisions. In an industry facing climate challenges and increasing demand for efficient food production, we build solutions that directly impact global food security.

Working Conditions & Perks
- Hybrid work model: 2 days onsite at our California headquarters, 3 days remote
- Flexible hours with core collaboration periods during business hours
- Competitive base salary plus annual performance bonus
- $5,000 annual learning budget for cloud certifications, specialized courses, and agricultural tech conferences
- Comprehensive health, dental, and vision benefits with 401(k) matching

Requirements (Must-Have)
- 3+ years of professional experience building and deploying machine learning models in production environments
- Proficiency in Python (Pandas, Scikit-learn, TensorFlow) and SQL for large-scale agricultural datasets
- Experience with AWS cloud infrastructure and data pipeline development for time-series sensor data
- Demonstrated ability to analyze weather patterns, soil moisture, and crop health metrics to inform actionable agricultural insights

Nice-to-Have
- Previous experience working with satellite imagery or IoT farm sensor data (e.g., soil moisture, yield monitors)
- Knowledge of agricultural economics models or regulatory frameworks for precision farming

Responsibilities
- Develop and maintain predictive models for crop yield estimation using historical weather, soil, and field data
- Clean, validate, and transform sensor data from distributed farm equipment into actionable analytics
- Collaborate with field engineers to integrate new sensor technologies into our platform's data ingestion pipeline
- Deploy and monitor machine learning models in AWS production environments with focus on low-latency agricultural applications

title: AI Engineer
description:
About Preston-Davis
Preston-Davis designs and operates scalable, cloud-native infrastructure for high-traffic web applications and SaaS platforms, focusing on reliability and performance for enterprises. We specialize in building synergized systems that integrate AI capabilities into production workflows while maintaining robust operational resilience. Our mission is to deliver infrastructure solutions that empower businesses to deploy intelligent features without compromising stability.

Working Conditions & Perks
Hybrid work model (2 days onsite in our Austin office, 3 days remote) with flexible hours
Competitive salary plus 10% annual performance bonus
$1,500/year learning budget for courses, certifications, and tooling
Comprehensive healthcare coverage including mental health support
401(k) with 60% employer match
Unlimited PTO with 10 days of paid sick leave

Requirements (Must-Have)
3+ years of production experience building and maintaining AI/ML systems
Proven proficiency in Python, with deep experience in ML pipelines (data ingestion, feature engineering, model training)
Hands-on experience deploying and monitoring production ML systems using Kubernetes and CI/CD pipelines
Strong background in cloud infrastructure (AWS or Azure) with containerization skills (Docker)
Experience optimizing infrastructure for cost and scalability in real-world environments

Nice-to-Have
Experience with TensorFlow Serving or similar production model serving frameworks
Knowledge of cloud-native architecture patterns for distributed AI workloads
Experience troubleshooting distributed systems and latency issues in production environments
Familiarity with observability tools like Prometheus, Grafana, or ELK stack

Responsibilities
Design and implement end-to-end ML pipelines for web applications using Python and cloud services
Deploy, scale, and monitor production AI models within Kubernetes clusters to ensure reliability and low latency
Collaborate with DevOps and product teams to integrate AI features into existing infrastructure while maintaining uptime
Troubleshoot and optimize performance bottlenecks in distributed AI systems, focusing on cost efficiency and scalability

title: Senior Data Scientist
description:
About Jones LLC
Jones LLC is a leading platform that helps brands and social media platforms establish high-impact viral partnerships to accelerate growth. We operate in the fast-paced digital marketing landscape, focusing on turning strategic alliances into shareable, high-engagement content that drives measurable results for clients. Our mission is to redefine how partnerships become viral through data-driven insights and real-time analytics.

Working Conditions & Perks
- Hybrid work model: 2 days onsite per week, 3 days remote
- Flexible work hours with core collaboration windows (10 AM–2 PM EST)
- Competitive base salary + annual performance bonus (target: 15% of base)
- $5,000 annual learning budget for courses, certifications, and tooling
- Comprehensive health, dental, and vision benefits including mental health coverage
- 401(k) with 6% company match up to $6,000 annually

Requirements (Must-Have)
- 5+ years of experience as a Data Scientist in product or engineering contexts, with direct ownership of machine learning model deployment
- Strong proficiency in Python and SQL, including experience with MLOps pipelines (e.g., MLflow, Docker)
- Proven track record of leading data projects that improved business metrics like conversion rates or engagement by 20%+ in previous roles
- Hands-on experience with large-scale data (10M+ records) and distributed systems (e.g., Apache Spark, Kafka)

Nice-to-Have
- Experience in social media marketing or viral content strategy, including A/B testing frameworks
- Knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS/GCP) for data storage and processing pipelines

Responsibilities
- Design and deploy machine learning models to predict engagement potential for new viral partnership initiatives
- Collaborate with product and marketing teams to define KPIs and build real-time dashboards for partnership performance
- Optimize data pipelines to process high-velocity social media interaction data (e.g., shares, comments, clicks)
- Analyze post-launch partnership performance to identify virality drivers and recommend iterative improvements

title: Jr. Data Scientist
description:
About Clay-Santana
Clay-Santana engineers next-generation network infrastructure for enterprise clients, specializing in low-latency, AI-enhanced systems that optimize real-time data flow across global networks. We bridge telecommunications and machine learning to deliver resilient, scalable solutions for high-stakes environments like financial markets and IoT ecosystems.

Working Conditions & Perks
Hybrid work model: 2 days onsite at our San Francisco office, 3 days remote  
Flexible hours with core collaboration window (10 AM–2 PM EST)  
Competitive base salary plus 15% annual performance bonus  
$5,000 annual learning budget for courses, conferences, and tool licenses  
Comprehensive health benefits including mental health support and 401(k) matching  
Professional development stipend for networking events and industry workshops

Requirements (Must-Have)
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Statistics, or related field  
1–2 years of hands-on experience with Python, SQL, and at least one ML framework (e.g., scikit-learn)  
Strong foundation in statistical analysis, hypothesis testing, and data validation techniques  
Proven ability to process and analyze structured network datasets (e.g., TCP/IP logs, latency metrics)

Nice-to-Have
Experience with network monitoring tools (e.g., Wireshark, NetFlow)  
Cloud infrastructure familiarity (AWS/GCP)  
Knowledge of basic network protocols (TCP, UDP, HTTP)  
Background in distributed systems or big data pipelines (e.g., Apache Spark)

Responsibilities
Collect, clean, and prepare network telemetry data using Python and SQL  
Build and validate ML models to detect anomalies and predict network failures  
Collaborate with network engineering teams to translate model outputs into actionable network configurations  
Document data pipelines and model performance metrics for cross-functional use  
Participate in weekly sprints to refine network optimization strategies using data insights  
Support A/B testing of network configurations with quantitative validation metrics

title: Machine Learning Engineer
description:
About Lawson Ltd
Lawson Ltd is an enterprise AI platform company that helps Fortune 500 organizations deploy scalable, production-ready machine learning solutions for complex operational challenges. We specialize in orchestrating end-to-end machine learning workflows across distributed systems, enabling real-time decisioning for high-stakes applications like supply chain optimization and predictive maintenance. Our mission is to bridge the gap between cutting-edge machine learning research and enterprise-grade operational efficiency through robust, maintainable systems.

Working Conditions & Perks
Hybrid work model: 2 days onsite in our San Francisco headquarters with flexible remote work for the remainder of the week. Competitive annual salary with performance-based bonus (target 15% of base) and equity grants. $4,500 annual learning budget for relevant courses, conferences, and tooling. Comprehensive health, dental, vision, and 401(k) plan with 50% company match. Unlimited PTO with 10 days of paid parental leave. Core collaboration window from 9 AM to 12 PM PST for urgent coordination.

Requirements (Must-Have)
3+ years building and deploying production ML systems with measurable outcomes (e.g., >10k user scale models). Proficiency in Python with PyTorch/TensorFlow for model development and productionization. Hands-on experience with MLOps pipelines using MLflow or similar tools for model versioning and deployment. Strong background in distributed systems, scalable data processing, and latency optimization for real-world workloads.

Nice-to-Have
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) for managed ML services and production deployments. Background in working with large-scale data (100TB+) and distributed computing frameworks like Spark or Dask. Knowledge of model monitoring, feature stores, and data versioning tools (e.g., Evidently, Feast). Familiarity with Kubernetes for containerized ML workflows.

Responsibilities
Design and implement end-to-end ML pipelines for data ingestion, feature engineering, model training, and production deployment. Monitor model performance in live environments, implement automated retraining triggers, and resolve drift issues within 24-hour SLAs. Collaborate with data engineers to optimize data processing pipelines for latency and cost efficiency. Maintain documentation for ML systems and coordinate with stakeholders to refine model requirements based on business metrics.

title: DevOps Engineer
description:
About Reyes and Sons
Reyes and Sons is a leading provider of distributed network solutions for modern cloud-native applications. We help enterprises build resilient, scalable networks across hybrid and multi-cloud environments. Our mission is to empower organizations with the infrastructure that enables seamless global connectivity and high-performance distributed systems.

Working Conditions & Perks
- Hybrid work model with two days in-office per week for collaboration
- Flexible work hours with asynchronous communication practices
- Competitive base salary and annual performance bonus
- $2,000 annual learning budget for courses, certifications, and tools
- Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance
- 401(k) plan with 50% company match and 10 days of paid time off

Requirements (Must-Have)
- 3+ years of hands-on DevOps experience in cloud environments (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
- Proficiency in Infrastructure as Code tools (Terraform or Ansible)
- Experience building and maintaining Kubernetes clusters and CI/CD pipelines
- Strong understanding of network fundamentals for distributed systems (TCP/IP, routing, DNS, etc.)
- Hands-on experience with monitoring and alerting systems (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana)
- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related field (or equivalent practical experience)

Nice-to-Have
- Experience with network automation tools (e.g., Ansible for network devices)
- Knowledge of edge computing and distributed network architectures
- Familiarity with cloud network services (e.g., AWS VPC, Azure Virtual Network)
- Experience with zero-trust security models

Responsibilities
- Design, implement, and maintain infrastructure as code for distributed network environments using Terraform.
- Monitor and optimize network performance and application health across distributed systems using Prometheus and Grafana.
- Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for network services and infrastructure changes to ensure reliability and rapid deployment.
- Troubleshoot and resolve production network issues, including distributed system latency and connectivity problems.
- Collaborate with network engineers and application developers to ensure seamless integration and deployment of distributed network services.
- Document and automate network configuration management processes to reduce manual intervention and improve consistency.

title: Product Manager
description:
About Coleman-Gonzales
Coleman-Gonzales is a global provider of intelligent cross-media bandwidth management solutions for enterprise-scale content delivery and digital advertising platforms. We help organizations optimize real-time data flow across video, audio, and interactive media to ensure seamless user experiences while maximizing network efficiency. Our mission is to solve critical bandwidth allocation challenges in today’s multi-platform media ecosystems through scalable, data-driven infrastructure.

Working Conditions & Perks
- Hybrid work model: 3 days onsite at our Austin, Texas headquarters and 2 days remote with flexible hours
- Competitive annual salary with performance-based bonuses and 15% profit-sharing
- $2,500 annual learning budget for courses, certifications, and industry conferences
- Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance with mental health support
- 401(k) plan with 6% company match and 20 days of paid parental leave
- Unlimited PTO with asynchronous work options for deep focus

Requirements (Must-Have)
- 5+ years of product management experience in tech or media products with documented impact on business metrics
- Proven proficiency in Agile tools (Jira, Confluence) and SQL for data analysis
- Direct experience optimizing bandwidth usage metrics in content delivery or advertising tech stacks
- Demonstrated ability to bridge technical constraints with business outcomes in high-stakes environments
- Working knowledge of CDNs, video streaming protocols (HLS/DASH), and real-time data pipelines

Nice-to-Have
- Hands-on experience with cloud infrastructure (AWS/Azure) and DevOps practices
- Background in video analytics or digital advertising tech stacks
- Experience leading cross-functional teams in bandwidth optimization projects

Responsibilities
- Own the product lifecycle for cross-media bandwidth optimization features from ideation to post-launch analysis
- Collaborate with engineering, data science, and design teams to implement real-time bandwidth monitoring solutions
- Analyze usage metrics to identify performance bottlenecks and quantify improvement opportunities
- Prioritize feature development based on business impact, technical complexity, and user behavior data
- Execute A/B tests to validate feature effectiveness and measure ROI on bandwidth efficiency gains
- Maintain technical documentation for product decisions and infrastructure integrations

title: UX Designer
description:
About Hudson-Blair
Hudson-Blair is a SaaS company helping mid-market enterprises streamline complex workflow automation and data integration. We build collaborative platforms that connect teams across sales, operations, and analytics, serving clients in finance, manufacturing, and healthcare. Our mission is to reduce operational friction without sacrificing security or scalability in the growing B2B digital transformation space.

Working Conditions & Perks
- Hybrid work model: 2 days onsite in our Chicago office, 3 days remote with flexible hours
- Competitive salary plus annual performance bonus based on team goals
- $10,000 annual learning budget for UX tools, certifications, and conference attendance
- Comprehensive health, dental, and vision benefits with 401(k) matching
- Results-based workflow: no micromanagement; focus on delivering client value through iterative design
- Unlimited PTO with 2 weeks for focused work blocks

Requirements (Must-Have)
- 3+ years designing B2B product interfaces for enterprise clients (e.g., workflow automation platforms)
- Proficiency in Figma for UI/UX design, prototyping, and component libraries
- Demonstrated experience conducting user research and creating user journeys for complex business workflows
- Strong collaboration skills with engineers and product teams to implement design systems
- Understanding of enterprise security requirements and compliance frameworks (e.g., GDPR, SOC 2)

Nice-to-Have
- Experience with enterprise data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI)
- Background in supporting sales or operations teams through UX implementation
- Knowledge of integration patterns with major B2B platforms (e.g., Salesforce, Slack)

Responsibilities
- Conduct user interviews and task analysis for enterprise clients to identify workflow pain points
- Design and prototype end-to-end workflow solutions using Figma, ensuring scalability and compliance
- Collaborate with engineering teams to translate designs into secure, production-ready features
- Create and maintain design systems aligned with Hudson-Blair’s security and integration standards
- Iterate on designs based on client feedback and performance metrics from pilot deployments

title: Jr. QA Engineer
description:
About Boyle-Robbins  
Boyle-Robbins is a mid-sized tech firm building scalable web applications for enterprise clients across finance and e-commerce. We focus on delivering reliable, cross-platform digital solutions that work seamlessly across devices and networks, helping businesses achieve consistent web readiness without technical debt. Our mission is to simplify complex digital transformation through pragmatic engineering practices.

Working Conditions & Perks  
Hybrid work model (2 days onsite, 3 days remote) with flexible scheduling around key project deadlines  
Competitive salary starting at $65,000 with annual performance bonus potential  
$2,000 annual learning stipend for certified courses (e.g., AWS, Selenium, or API testing)  
Comprehensive health insurance, 401(k) matching, and 10 paid days of vacation  
Remote work flexibility with dedicated project hours for syncs  
Unlimited PTO after 90 days

Requirements (Must-Have)  
Familiarity with at least one programming language (JavaScript, Python, or Java) for basic automation  
Experience with API testing tools (e.g., Postman) or web automation frameworks (e.g., Selenium)  
Understanding of CI/CD pipelines and version control systems (Git)  
Ability to document test cases clearly and report bugs with repro steps in Jira

Nice-to-Have  
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS or Azure) for deployment testing  
Knowledge of mobile testing frameworks (e.g., Appium) for cross-device validation  
Experience in agile environments with sprint planning and daily standups  
Basic familiarity with performance testing tools (e.g., Lighthouse)

Responsibilities  
Execute manual test cases for new web features using Jira to track defects  
Run automated test scripts on staging environments to validate API endpoints and UI flows  
Collaborate with developers to prioritize and resolve critical bugs before releases  
Document test results and automation improvements in Confluence for team transparency  
Support continuous integration pipelines by validating build quality pre-deployment  
Analyze test failure patterns to identify recurring issues in web applications

title: Full Stack Developer
description:
About Deleon-Aguirre
Deleon-Aguirre is a technology consultancy specializing in helping mid-sized businesses monetize their web infrastructure through scalable backend solutions for e-commerce and SaaS platforms. We focus on turning technical web readiness into direct revenue streams by building secure, high-performance systems that handle payments, subscriptions, and user data compliance. Our mission is to reduce the technical barriers for businesses to generate sustainable online revenue without compromising security or scalability.

Working Conditions & Perks
- Remote-first model with two hybrid days per week in our San Francisco office for collaboration and team events
- Flexible schedule with core syncs (9 AM - 12 PM EST) and full autonomy over personal work hours
- Competitive annual salary starting at $120,000 plus 15% annual performance bonus
- $5,000 annual learning budget for courses, certifications, and tooling
- Comprehensive benefits including health insurance, 401(k) with 4% company match, 20 paid days off, and parental leave

Requirements (Must-Have)
- 3+ years of professional full-stack development experience with explicit focus on backend monetization systems (e.g., payment processing, subscription management)
- Proficiency in Python or Node.js and one frontend framework (React or Angular)
- Hands-on experience with AWS/GCP cloud infrastructure and CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions or Jenkins)
- Demonstrated ability to design and optimize database schemas for high-volume transaction processing
- Experience integrating payment gateways (Stripe, PayPal) with PCI-DSS compliance requirements
- Strong understanding of RESTful APIs and microservices architecture for scalable revenue systems

Nice-to-Have
- Experience with Docker and infrastructure-as-code (Terraform)
- Knowledge of monitoring tools (Prometheus, Grafana) for production systems
- Background in e-commerce or SaaS product development

Responsibilities
- Build and deploy backend services for payment processing, user subscriptions, and revenue tracking
- Integrate third-party monetization tools while ensuring PCI-DSS compliance and data security
- Collaborate with frontend teams to implement seamless API interactions and responsive user experiences
- Optimize database queries and API performance to meet strict client SLAs for transactional systems
- Maintain comprehensive documentation and version-controlled code for monetization services
- Participate in sprint planning and code reviews to ensure high-quality, maintainable revenue systems
